repositories.name: user-service-api
repositories.customReviewInstructions: We strictly enforce SOLID principles and PEP8 compliance. Do not allow hardcoded secrets or raw query string configurations under any circumstance.

repositories.name: auth-gateway
repositories.customReviewInstructions: Ensure complete token lifecycle hygiene is checked. Highlight any vulnerabilities that might permit token leak or XSS injection.

pullRequests.title: feat: add user register API with dynamic query parameters
pullRequests.description: Adds a fast registration endpoint. Implemented raw database lookup using python string interpolation to query database rapidly. Needs quick merge.

pullRequests.title: fix: bypass token expiration checks for offline staging testing
pullRequests.description: Temporarily disables JWT expire checks so offline testing remains functional on staging servers. Please do not merge into prod.

reviews.summary: The code adds dynamic registration but introduces a **Critical Security Vulnerability** (SQL Injection) due to using string formatting instead of parameterized query arguments. Testing coverage is also absent for validation helper methods.
reviews.positiveObservations:
• Code is cleanly modularized into a single route function file.
• Routing patterns use standardized RESTful endpoints.
reviews.criticalIssuesSummary: • **SQL Injection Vector**: Raw values parsed from user forms are formatted directly into PostgreSQL queries inside `UserService.py` on line 18.
reviews.recommendedFixes:
1. Replace dynamic query string formatting (`f'SELECT * FROM users WHERE email={email}'`) with proper parameterized cursor arguments (`db.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s', (email,))`).
2. Add test cases executing registration requests with invalid email patterns and special SQL injection characters.

issues.title: SQL Injection Susceptibility
issues.description: The parameter `email` is concatenated directly into SQL statement: `query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email='{email}'"`. Attackers can escape this quoting context.
issues.suggestion: Use database execute parameters: `cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s", (email,))`

issues.title: Missing Exception Boundary
issues.description: Executing cursor query can fail if connection pool gets exhausted or unique constraints break, resulting in unhandled 500 error on frontend clients.
issues.suggestion: Wrap db cursor transactions in an explicit `try ... except DatabaseError` block and roll back transactions safely.

comments.body:
### ⚠️ **SQL Injection Vulnerability** [Critical - Security]

Raw string interpolation interpolates unvalidated variables straight into SQL.

💡 **Suggestion:**
```python
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s", (email,))
```

reviews.summary: Disabling token expiration verification leaves the system open to **Replay Attacks** and bypass authorization mechanisms. This is a severe threat even inside staging networks.
reviews.criticalIssuesSummary: • **Hardcoded JWT verification bypass**: The validation helper completely skips decoding boundaries.
reviews.recommendedFixes: 1. Instead of bypassing the check completely inside the core validation function, configure an environment variables based fallback context where an explicit sandbox signature secret is utilized for developers.

issues.title: JWT Authentication Bypass
issues.description: The line `if (env === 'staging') return true;` effectively short-circuits key signature validation.
issues.suggestion: Remove this bypass. Always validate claims and token signatures regardless of environment strings.

comments.body:
### ⚠️ **Authentication Bypass** [Critical - Security]

Bypassing cryptographic signatures inside staging results in vulnerability leakage to testing targets.

💡 **Suggestion:**
```javascript
// Rely on valid staging credentials instead of a wildcard staging string bypass
const payload = jwt.verify(token, STAGING_SECRET);
```

reviews.summary:
### 💡 Sandbox Mock Review
Configure your **GEMINI_API_KEY** in the secrets settings to run live AI-powered PR reviews.

This is a mock analysis for preview demonstration purposes. Your code is clean but SQL injection risk still persists on line 18.
reviews.positiveObservations: • Added safe return boundary checking on empty result rows.
reviews.criticalIssuesSummary: • Hardcoded SQL injection susceptibility remains unresolved in string query format.
reviews.recommendedFixes: 1. Upgrade DB connection wrapper to use parameterized tuple variables.

issues.title: Dynamic SQL Concatenation
issues.description: Direct formatting of query strings makes parsing boundaries insecure against input-based SQL injections.
issues.suggestion: Use parameterized execute arguments instead of interpolation.

comments.body:
### ⚠️ **SQL Injection susceptibility** [Critical - Security]

String formatting on raw queries poses injection hazard.

💡 **Suggestion:**
```python
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s", (email,))
```
